run_summary: Screen 2 has no open positions to review. Two new flags arrived, both OVERDONE classification — structurally ineligible for a long-only book. Both are SKIPped.

entries.name: Modine Manufacturing
entries.thesis: The pending Gentherm RMT transaction introduces specific, filings-acknowledged execution and distraction risks (employee, customer, supplier) in the same Performance Technologies segment whose end markets the 10-K already described as deteriorating. Management commentary around a quarter this close to a complex spin tends to surface caution, transaction costs, and conservative segment outlooks — risks the market may be underweighting if focused on the data center growth story.
entries.what_kills: Data Center Cooling outperformance (it was 25% of FY25 revenue and growing fast) large enough to dominate the narrative; clean reaffirmation of transaction timeline with no negative segment surprises; PT segment stabilizing rather than deteriorating.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this. Classification is a real Screen 2 signal (pending RMT transaction introduces filings-disclosed distraction and segment-softness risks in Performance Technologies), but the directional call is bearish and cannot be held as a long position in this structure.

entries.name: Asana
entries.thesis: Filings show retention deterioration (NRR below 100% on the highest-spend cohorts) coexisting with a heavy narrative pivot to AI/Agentic Enterprise that is not yet quantified as revenue contribution. Combined with executive departures and a disclosed security flaw in an AI-adjacent feature, the next print is more likely to surface continued NRR pressure and elevated AI investment than to deliver an upside catalyst — particularly if guidance was raised in Q3 on AI optimism.
entries.what_kills: NRR rebounds above 100%; a specific, quantified AI Studio/AI Teammates revenue contribution disclosure; clean reaffirmation of FY27 with strong large-customer growth (817 over-100K customers expanding).
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this. The filings case is specific and grounded: NRR below 100% on high-spend cohorts, senior executive departures (COO, GC both Dec 31, 2025), and unmonetized AI product pivot. The thesis that the next print surfaces continued NRR pressure rather than an AI upside catalyst is sound, but the classification is bearish and structurally cannot be expressed as a long position.
